title: Springfield Firearms Task Force

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

The Springfield Firearms Task Force, publicly announced in Fall 2024 by the Springfield Police Department and the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms, and Explosives (ATF) as a joint initiative to investigate and prosecute firearm-related violence in Springfield and surrounding counties. 

Public statements described the task force as targeting individuals involved in firearm-related violence, including straw purchasers, felons in possession of firearms, drug traffickers possessing firearms, and individuals possessing firearm conversion devices (“switches”), and as utilizing crime gun intelligence and NIBIN technology. Officials also stated the task force would measure results and foster trust and legitimacy in the community.

I am requesting the following records:

1. Establishment and Structure

The Memorandum of Understanding (MOU), intergovernmental agreement, or other written agreement between the Springfield Police Department and ATF establishing the Springfield Firearms Task Force.
Any written charter, operational plan, or internal document outlining the task force’s mission, enforcement priorities, or scope of authority.
Any amendments or revisions to the above documents.

2. Personnel

The number of Springfield Police Department officers assigned to the Springfield Firearms Task Force since its creation.
Documentation indicating whether those officers are deputized or designated as federal task force officers.

3. Enforcement Activity (Aggregate Totals Only)

From the task force’s creation in Fall 2024 to the present:

Total number of arrests resulting from task force operations.
Total number of firearms seized.
Total number of investigations involving alleged straw purchasers.
Total number of investigations involving unlawful firearm possession by felons.
Total number of investigations involving firearm conversion devices (“switches”).
Total number of cases referred to federal prosecutors.
Total number of cases referred to state or county prosecutors.
I am requesting aggregate totals only and am not seeking individual case files, investigative reports, or records related to specific ongoing investigations.

4. Measurement of Results

Any internal reports, summaries, presentations, or evaluations assessing the effectiveness or results of the Springfield Firearms Task Force.
Any documents describing how the Springfield Police Department measures the task force’s performance or outcomes.

5. Clearance Rates

Homicide clearance rates for calendar years 2022, 2023, 2024, and 2025 to date.

Nonfatal shooting clearance rates for calendar years 2022, 2023, 2024, and 2025 to date.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Josiah Bates

title: CIA: MDR: Spain and a Weakened UCD: Downhill Toward an Election

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to Section 3.5 of Executive Order 13526 and the implementing regulations of the Central Intelligence Agency (32 C.F.R. Part 1908), I hereby submit this request for Mandatory Declassification Review (MDR) of the following document:

Title: “Spain and a Weakened UCD: Downhill Toward an Election”
Document Identifier: CIA-RDP83-00857R000100030002-7
Date of Document: July 1982
Originating Directorate: Directorate of Intelligence (EUR 82-10069 / CR 82-13764)
Online Location: https://www.cia.gov/readingroom/docs/CIA-RDP83-00857R000100030002-7.pdf

I respectfully request a line-by-line review of all redacted portions of this document, which was partially released through the CIA CREST database and approved for release on August 12, 2008. While the Agency’s prior review resulted in a meaningful partial disclosure, a substantial number of redactions remain throughout the document. I believe that changed circumstances — discussed below — now warrant full or substantially expanded disclosure of the withheld material.
I. The 25X1 Exemptions: Changed Circumstances Regarding the 1981 Coup Attempt

A significant portion of the redactions in this document bear the “25X1” exemption notation, which protects intelligence sources and methods. Many of these redactions appear in sections directly related to the February 23, 1981 coup attempt (“23-F”) — the effort by Lt. Col. Antonio Tejero Molina and associated military figures to seize the Spanish parliament — as well as to the internal deliberations of the Union of the Democratic Center (UCD) and Spanish military attitudes toward a potential Socialist government.
It is respectfully submitted that the passage of over four decades, combined with the Spanish government’s own recent transparency efforts regarding the events of 23-F, substantially diminishes any remaining justification for withholding this material on sources-and-methods grounds. The Spanish government has in recent years declassified and released significant documentation related to the coup attempt, and the historical record of that event is now extensively documented in both academic and journalistic sources. The operational sensitivity of CIA collection efforts related to Spanish internal politics in 1981–1982 cannot reasonably be said to remain intact after more than forty years.

I accordingly request that the Agency give particular attention, in its line-by-line review, to redactions in the main analytical text and in the section titled “The Coup Attempt,” with a view toward releasing any material whose continued classification cannot be affirmatively justified under current standards.

II. The 25X6 Exemptions: Deceased Foreign Nationals

Several redactions in Appendix B (Biographical Profiles) of this document bear the “25X6” exemption notation, which protects information provided in confidence by a foreign government or international organization. These redactions appear specifically in the biographical profiles of the following three individuals:

1.  Manuel Fraga Iribarne, President of the Popular Alliance Party — deceased January 15, 2012.
2.  Francisco José Fernández-Ordóñez, Leader of Democratic Action — deceased June 26, 1992.
3.  Adolfo Suárez González, Former Prime Minister of Spain and UCD founder — deceased March 23, 2014.

All three individuals are now deceased. The privacy and diplomatic sensitivities that may have originally justified the 25X6 exemption with respect to their personal profiles have been substantially diminished by the passage of time and the deaths of all subjects involved. Adolfo Suárez González, in particular, served as Spain’s first democratically elected Prime Minister and is now a major historical figure; the fullest possible historical record of assessments of his character and political conduct is plainly in the public interest.

I respectfully ask that the Agency assess whether the foreign government or organization whose confidential information underlies the 25X6 exemptions would consent to release, given that the subjects of those assessments are deceased and that the events described are now historical in nature. Should that consent be obtainable, I urge its pursuit as part of this review.

Thank you for the agency's commitment to transparency.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

title: FBI: 1981 Spanish Coup figure Antonio Tejero

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FBI: 1981 Spanish Coup figure Antonio Tejero
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am seeking any and all records maintained by the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) concerning Antonio Tejero Molina (also known as Antonio Tejero), the former Spanish Civil Guard lieutenant colonel who led the attempted coup d'état in Spain on February 23, 1981 (23-F).

Proof of Death and Public Interest
The subject of this request, Antonio Tejero, died on February 25, 2026, at the age of 93. His death has been widely reported by international news media, including The Guardian (link: https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/feb/25/spanish-officer-led-1981-coup-dies-day-documents-declassified-antonio-tejero). As the subject is deceased, his individual privacy interests are significantly diminished, and all responsive records are now eligible for public release. There is a substantial public interest in understanding the U.S. government’s contemporary observations and intelligence regarding the 1981 coup and Tejero’s subsequent activities.

Scope of Search
I request that the FBI conduct a search of the Central Records System (CRS) and any other relevant indices for all documents, including but not limited to:

Investigative reports, 1-A envelopes, and "cross-references."

Intelligence assessments regarding the February 23, 1981, coup attempt.

Communications between the FBI and Spanish law enforcement regarding Tejero’s imprisonment or radical right-wing movements in Spain.

Specific Request for Madrid Legal Attaché (Legat) Records
In addition to a search of FBI Headquarters (FBIHQ) files, I specifically request a search of files generated, received, or held by the FBI Legal Attaché (Legat) office in Madrid, Spain. Given Tejero’s role in a major domestic Spanish uprising, it is highly probable that the Madrid Legat maintained specific intelligence files, liaison communications with the Guardia Civil and Policía Nacional, and situational reports that may not be fully duplicated in HQ files.

As you know, the Federal Bureau of Investigation is required to search the records stored at NARA’s Washington Records Center in Suitland, MD, but still technically under the FBI’s control. Please also conduct a physical search of records at NARA's Washington Records Center in Suitland, for any records or documents related to Antonio Tejero. 

If the bureau cannot fulfill certain parts of this request or certain portions must be redacted please provide any reasonable segregable portion of a requested record after removing or redacting those portions claimed to be exempt, explain in writing the justification for redacting the record, indicate the extent of any redactions on the portion of the record which is made available or published and where technically feasible indicate on the redacted portion itself the specific exemption(s) claimed.

Thank you for the bureau's commitment to transparency.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

title: PD Settlement Agreements

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: PD Settlement Agreements
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to New Hampshire Right to Know law, I hereby request the following records:

 A copy of every agreement to settle a lawsuit, threatened lawsuit, or other claim against this agency over the past 10 years. This covers all agreements that would fall under New Hampshire RSA 91-A:4 (Right-to-Know Law), VI: "Every agreement to settle a lawsuit against a governmental unit, threatened lawsuit, or other claim, entered into by any political subdivision or its insurer, shall be kept on file at the municipal clerk's office and made available for public inspection for a period of no less than 10 years from the date of settlement."

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jonathan Van Fleet
Concord Monitor
1 Monitor Drive
Concord NH 03301
603.369.3303

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting an inspection or copies of public records related to a specific police action that occurred on January 22nd, 2026 at approximately 1:30 PM.

The incident took place at 535 Alameda Street, Los Angeles CA, 90012, outside of the Metropolitan Detention Center. At least 11 LAPD cars pulled up to the loading dock for the Metropolitan Detention Center on Alameda street. Six or seven cars then parked right outside of the loading dock in the driveway of the MDC, while some parked along Alameda. title: ALPR Data Sharing, Vendor Contracts, and Query Logs (San Luis Obispo County Sheriff)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: ALPR Data Sharing, Vendor Contracts, and Query Logs (San Luis Obispo County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. The current written ALPR Privacy and Usage Policy as required by Vehicle Code §2413(b), including all versions in effect since January 1, 2022.
2. All contracts, MOUs, data-sharing agreements, or other agreements with any ALPR vendor, including Flock Safety and Vigilant Solutions/Motorola Solutions, since January 1, 2022.
3. All contracts, MOUs, or data-sharing agreements with any federal agency — including ICE, CBP/Border Patrol, HSI, DEA, ATF, FBI, or any U.S. military branch — or any out-of-state law enforcement agency, related to ALPR data access.
4. A complete list of all agencies or entities authorized to query the SLO Sheriff's ALPR database, or that have been authorized to do so since January 1, 2022.
5. All audit logs from the date the SLO County Sheriff's Office first activated any ALPR system or vendor platform (including Flock Safety and Vigilant Solutions/Motorola Solutions) through the date of your response to this request, showing all ALPR database queries, including requesting agency name, date/time, and any available purpose or case number fields. Personal vehicle identifying data may be redacted. If providing the full contract period is deemed burdensome, please provide logs from January 1, 2024, through the date of response as a minimum, and specify the full date range of available records.
6. All notifications received from ALPR vendors — including Flock Safety — about unauthorized access, configuration errors, data breaches, or changes to any National Lookup, Statewide Lookup, or similar settings.
7. All notifications, security alerts, or vendor communications related to password theft, account compromises, or unauthorized system access.
8. Any internal communications since January 1, 2022, regarding the Sheriff's ALPR program's compliance with California privacy law or the Attorney General's enforcement actions against other agencies.
9. Any correspondence between the SLO County Sheriff's Office and the California Attorney General's Office regarding ALPR compliance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Debra Appelbaum

title: Request to Polk County Sheriff for Documents and Training on Dealing with Individuals with Autism

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Request to Polk County Sheriff for Documents and Training on Dealing with Individuals with Autism
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting access to documents held by the Polk County Sheriff's Office related to their training and procedures for dealing with individuals with autism.

I am aware of the Polk County Sheriff's Office's Autism Decal program, which provides a way for individuals with autism and their caregivers to inform law enforcement officers of their condition. I am interested in any records, reports, memoranda, emails, or other documents related to the development and implementation of this program, including any policies, guidelines, or procedures related to the program.

Specifically, I am seeking any records, reports, memoranda, emails, or other documents related to the Polk County Sheriff's Office's training and procedures for dealing with individuals with autism, including any policies, guidelines, or procedures related to this training or procedures. I am also requesting any communications between the Polk County Sheriff's Office and other law enforcement agencies or government entities related to the training or procedures for dealing with individuals with autism.

Additionally, I am interested in any records, reports, memoranda, emails, or other documents related to any incidents involving individuals with autism and the Polk County Sheriff's Office, including any use of force or arrests.

I want to emphasize that I do not consent to the withholding, deletion, or redaction of any responsive records or documents, except in cases where the Polk County Sheriff's Office can provide a specific justification for why the records are exempt from disclosure under the Florida Public Records Law. Please provide me with any non-exempt records or documents in their entirety, including any segregable portions that may be released.

Furthermore, I hereby provide notice that any attempt to withhold, redact, or delete responsive records or documents without providing a specific justification for why they are exempt from disclosure will be met with a complaint and legal action to compel the Polk County Sheriff's Office to provide access to the requested records in accordance with the Florida Public Records Law.

In support of this request, I would like to reference relevant case law that affirms the public's right to access government records. One key case is Times Publishing Co. v. City of St. Petersburg, 558 So. 2d 487 (Fla. 2d DCA 1990), which held that the Florida Public Records Law reflects a "strong public policy in favor of access to public records." Another important case is News-Press Publishing Co. v. Carlson, 410 So. 2d 546 (Fla. 2d DCA 1982), which held that the Florida Public Records Law applies to all "writings made or received by an agency in connection with official business."

If any responsive records or documents are exempt from disclosure, I request that you provide me with a justification for each exemption relied upon.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Zackary DeCicco

Preliminary search uncovered no responsive records, and I am not sure that the current CBA discussions between the WNBA and the WNBPA found the NLRB yet. Not all CBA negotiations are before the NLRB; it is more common when the parties find they are at an impasse. I wanted to reach out to you to see if you had more specific information or better information that me before reaching that conclusion. 

I would be happy to discuss your request with you and explain the information needed.  Please let me know if you are available for a phone call or reply to this email with additional information that would allow the FOIA Branch to conduct a search for responsive records. 

If you have any questions, please contact me, Joseph Mullaney at (202) 273-3863 or by e-mail at joseph.mullaney@nlrb.gov. 

Sincerely, 
Joseph Mullaney 
FOIA Attorney 

FOIA Branch 
National Labor Relations Board

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Any contracts between Malden Public Schools and Amplify Education, as well as the approved Malden Public Schools budgets from FY 2020, FY 2021, FY 2022, FY 2023, FY 2024, and FY 2025.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Dear  Avieana Rivera : 

We have received your public Records request for Malden. This record is being assigned to appropriate department. The department reviewer will review the request and create an effort estimate to create the response. Per state mandate, for Malden, if the effort estimate is more than 2 hours, we will create a proposed cost estimate and will email such estimate to your attention. Once you approve the amount and send the payment, we will start working on your request. 

In case the estimate is less than 2 hours, we will proceed to work on the response and will send you the response once the work is completed. Please also note that state mandate allows us to complete and send the response within 10 business days. We will do our due diligence towards this effort. However, the due date may be extended for any of the following reasons: 
1.   Any clarification that is required from you towards this request will stop the clock. The clock will be resumed once the proper clarification is received. 
2.   Payment of estimated effort – The time it takes to receive the due amount will be added to the original due date. 
3.    If in the opinion of the reviewer it is determined that original due date cannot be met despite reasonable effort, the due date shall be extended based on reviewer’s judgment. You will be promptly notified in such case with revised due date.
